task_completion.description: Create the conditions CSV file with appropriate columns and consonant stimuli.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A spreadsheet editor, text editor, or terminal output showing the creation of 'nback_conditions.csv' containing 'letter' and 'is_target' columns, populated with consonant letters and target indicators (e.g., 1/0 or True/False) across multiple rows.

task_completion.description: Save the PsychoPy experiment to the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: PsychoPy Builder interface is visible, and the window title or a save dialog indicates the file is named 'nback_experiment.psyexp' and saved in the '/home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/' directory.

task_completion.description: Set up the trial routine with stimulus and response components.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The PsychoPy Builder shows a routine containing at least a Text component (to display the consonant) and a Keyboard component (to capture the participant's match response).

task_completion.description: Wrap the trial routine in a loop connected to the conditions file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The PsychoPy flow panel shows a loop around the trial routine. The loop properties dialog should show 'nback_conditions.csv' loaded in the conditions field.

task_completion.description: Implement the adaptive n-back logic using a Code component.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Code component properties window is open in PsychoPy, showing Python or JS code that initializes n=2, tracks accuracy over the last 10 trials, increases n if accuracy > 85%, and decreases n if accuracy < 55%.

integrity.description: The agent used the PsychoPy Builder GUI to construct the experiment.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent actively clicking and typing within the PsychoPy Builder application, rather than just writing a raw .psyexp XML/JSON file in a text editor.

integrity.description: The adaptive logic in the Code component is genuinely implemented.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Code component contains actual programming logic (variables, arrays/lists for tracking the last 10 trials, and if/else statements for the 85% and 55% thresholds) rather than dummy text or comments.

integrity.description: The agent created the files locally rather than downloading pre-made solutions.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of the agent using curl, wget, or a browser to download a completed 'nback_experiment.psyexp' or 'nback_conditions.csv' from an external URL.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Output experiment file must be exactly: /home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/nback_experiment.psyexp. Conditions file must be exactly: /home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/conditions/nback_conditions.csv. The conditions file must have >= 30 rows, include 'letter' and 'is_target' columns, and have a target rate between 25% and 45%.
